history: The client has a longstanding pattern of being self-critical and is used to maintaining control and achieving goals. They are currently struggling with job searching and experiencing ongoing conflicts in personal relationships and family dynamics, particularly involving a person named Ivan. These challenges appear to intensify their self-critical thinking and may contribute to ruminative patterns, as suggested by the report's emphasis on negative self-evaluation and repeated focus on perceived shortcomings.
intermediate_belief: If I don't succeed in finding a job or resolving conflicts with Ivan, it means I am not in control and I am failing as a person. I must always achieve my goals and keep things under control, or others will see me as inadequate. If I make mistakes or things go wrong, it reflects badly on who I am.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I keep failing at job searching and can't fix things with Ivan, it proves that I am hopeless and nothing will ever get better for me. My efforts don't matter because I can't change my situation.
coping_strategies: The client tends to avoid or withdraw from job search activities and social interactions to manage distress, as well as procrastinate due to fear of rejection or failure. They may also seek reassurance from others or engage in excessive planning and perfectionism to regain a sense of control, but these strategies provide only temporary relief and reinforce negative patterns.
situation: Experiencing difficulties with job searching. Dealing with ongoing relationship and family conflicts involving a person named Ivan.
auto_thought: I'm never going to find a job, and I can't fix things with Ivan. This just proves that I'm failing at everything right now.
behavior: I put off applying for jobs or avoid looking at job postings. I withdraw from conversations with Ivan or family members to avoid more conflict. Sometimes I over-plan or try to control small things to feel less helpless.
